mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which compounds corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <45% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects tightening supply-side capacity and rising utilization rates, which compresses short-term volatility and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 230.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 230.0 over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate profit margins, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, which depresses corporate earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where INDPRO drops below 95.0 over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.
